entities.name: TRICHODORUS REDUNCUS
entities.description: A new species of nematode isolated from groundnut fields in Vietnam.

entities.name: TYLENCHORHYNCHUS (DIVITTUS) DISPERSUS
entities.description: A new species of nematode isolated from groundnut fields in Vietnam.

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The host plant from which the nematode species were isolated.

entities.name: NGHE AN PROVINCE
entities.description: The province in Vietnam where the soil samples were collected.

entities.name: VIETNAM
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted.

entities.name: TYLENCHORHYNCHUS (DIVITTUS) DIVITTATUS
entities.description: A nematode species closely related to Tylenchorhynchus (Divittus) dispersus.

entities.name: ONCHIOSTYLE
entities.description: A morphological feature used to identify T. reduncus, measuring 36-40 µm in males and 37-40 µm in females.

entities.name: VENTROMEDIAN CERVICAL PAPILLAE
entities.description: Morphological feature of T. reduncus males located between the nerve ring and excretory pore.

entities.name: SPICULES
entities.description: Morphological feature used for identification in both T. reduncus and T. (D.) dispersus.

entities.name: GUBERNACULUM
entities.description: Morphological feature of T. reduncus males with a dorsally recurved proximal region.

entities.name: BODY LENGTH
entities.description: Morphological attribute of T. reduncus females, measuring 0.62-0.72 mm.

entities.name: VULVA POSITION
entities.description: Morphological attribute of T. reduncus females, located at 55-57 percent of the body.

entities.name: VAGINAL SCLEROTIZATION
entities.description: Morphological feature of T. reduncus females appearing as round to roughly triangular pieces.

entities.name: CAUDAL PORES
entities.description: Morphological feature of T. reduncus females located almost terminally.

entities.name: STYLET
entities.description: Morphological feature of T. (D.) dispersus, measuring 18-20 µm, used to distinguish it from T. (D.) divittatus.

entities.name: FEMALE TAIL DEPRESSION
entities.description: Morphological feature of T. (D.) dispersus located on the dorsal side of the tail.

entities.name: PHASMIDS
entities.description: Morphological feature of T. (D.) dispersus located anterior to the middle of the tail.
